name: Early-boot NX Protection
description: The UEFI system can set up memory attributes at boot which prevent common exploits from running. For instance, the stack can be marked as non-executable to prevent running any malicious code that can be written to the stack.
In the same way, writing to sections that are read-only can also be prevented, making the system more secure from attackers.
Distribution vendors should tag the bootloader as NX compatible, i.e. compiled with Data Execution Prevention (DEP).
failure-impact: The attacker is able to run arbitrary executable code from the stack, or write to memory that is supposed to be read-only.

failure-results.not-enabled: the bootloader is not marked as NX compatible

success-results.enabled: the bootloader is compiled as NX compatible

resolution: Contact your OS vendor, who may be able to issue a bootloader update.
